name: C4 Code Level
description: Level 4 — class structure of a single component (Order Service), showing dependencies and interfaces.
code:
classDiagram
  direction TB

  class OrderService {
    -repo: OrderRepository
    -payment: PaymentGateway
    -events: EventBus
    -inventory: InventoryClient
    +createOrder(req: CreateOrderRequest) Order
    +cancelOrder(id: UUID) void
    +getOrder(id: UUID) Order
    -validateStock(lines: List~OrderLine~) void
  }

  class Order {
    +id: UUID
    +customerId: UUID
    +status: OrderStatus
    +lines: List~OrderLine~
    +total: Money
    +place() void
    +cancel() void
    +addLine(line: OrderLine) void
  }

  class OrderLine {
    +productId: UUID
    +quantity: int
    +unitPrice: Money
    +lineTotal() Money
  }

  class OrderStatus {
    <<enumeration>>
    PENDING
    CONFIRMED
    SHIPPED
    DELIVERED
    CANCELLED
  }

  class OrderRepository {
    <<interface>>
    +save(order: Order) Order
    +findById(id: UUID) Order
    +findByCustomer(id: UUID) List~Order~
  }

  class PaymentGateway {
    <<interface>>
    +charge(orderId: UUID, amount: Money) PaymentResult
    +refund(paymentId: UUID) void
  }

  class EventBus {
    <<interface>>
    +publish(event: DomainEvent) void
  }

  class InventoryClient {
    <<interface>>
    +reserve(productId: UUID, qty: int) boolean
    +release(productId: UUID, qty: int) void
  }

  OrderService --> OrderRepository : uses
  OrderService --> PaymentGateway : uses
  OrderService --> EventBus : publishes to
  OrderService --> InventoryClient : checks
  OrderService ..> Order : creates
  Order *-- OrderLine : contains
  Order --> OrderStatus : has

name: Incident Response Runbook
description: SEV-1/2/3 triage, war room, investigation, fix verification, and PIR process.
code:
flowchart TD
  Alert["Alert Fired\nPagerDuty / OpsGenie"] --> Ack["On-call Engineer\nAcknowledges"]
  Ack --> Sev{"Severity?"}

  Sev -->|SEV-1 Customer Impact| War["Open War Room\nZoom + Slack"]
  Sev -->|SEV-2 Degraded| Inv["Investigate"]
  Sev -->|SEV-3 Minor| Ticket["Create Ticket\n& Monitor"]

  War --> IC["Assign Incident Commander"]
  IC --> Diag["Diagnose\nLogs + Traces + Metrics"]
  Inv --> Diag

  Diag --> Root{"Root Cause\nFound?"}
  Root -->|Yes| Fix["Apply Fix / Rollback\n/ Feature Flag Off"]
  Root -->|No| Esc["Escalate to\nDomain Expert"]
  Esc --> Diag

  Fix --> Verify["Verify Resolution\nMonitor 10 min"]
  Verify --> OK{"Resolved?"}
  OK -->|Yes| Comms["Post Resolution\nCommunication"]
  OK -->|No| Diag

  Comms --> PIR["Post-Incident Review\nwithin 48h"]
  PIR --> Actions["Track Action Items"]
  Ticket --> Actions

  classDef sev1 fill: #dc262620,stroke: #dc2626
  classDef sev2 fill: #d9770620,stroke: #d97706
  classDef process fill: #2563eb20,stroke: #2563eb
  classDef good fill: #05996020,stroke: #059669
  class Alert,War,IC sev1
  class Sev,Root,OK sev2

name: OAuth 2.0 PKCE Flow
description: Full PKCE authorization code flow: browser, auth server, resource server, token store.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor User
  participant Browser
  participant AS as Authorization Server
  participant RS as Resource Server
  participant TS as Token Store

  User->>Browser: Click Sign In
  Browser->>Browser: Generate code_verifier + code_challenge (S256)
  Browser->>AS: GET /authorize?response_type=code&code_challenge=...&scope=openid profile
  AS->>User: Render login + MFA prompt
  User->>AS: Submit credentials + OTP
  AS->>AS: Validate, create auth_code (TTL 60s)
  AS->>Browser: 302 to callback?code=auth_code&state=...
  Browser->>AS: POST /token {code, code_verifier, client_id}
  AS->>AS: Verify code_challenge == S256(code_verifier)
  AS->>TS: Store hashed refresh_token (TTL 90d)
  AS->>Browser: access_token JWT (15min) + refresh_token
  Browser->>RS: GET /api/resource Authorization: Bearer JWT
  RS->>RS: Verify JWT signature + expiry locally
  RS-->>Browser: 200 OK + data
  Browser->>User: Render protected content

  Note over Browser,AS: PKCE prevents authorization code injection attacks

name: Payment Processing Flow
description: End-to-end payment: customer, merchant, payment gateway, card network, bank.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor Customer
  participant Merchant
  participant Gateway as Payment Gateway
  participant Network as Card Network
  participant IssuerBank as Issuer Bank
  participant Ledger

  Customer->>Merchant: Submit payment (card + CVV)
  Merchant->>Gateway: POST /charge {amount, currency, card_token}
  Gateway->>Gateway: Tokenise PAN (PCI DSS)
  Gateway->>Network: Authorization Request (ISO 8583)
  Network->>IssuerBank: Forward auth request
  IssuerBank->>IssuerBank: Check balance, fraud score, 3DS if needed
  IssuerBank-->>Network: Auth response + approval_code
  Network-->>Gateway: Forwarded response
  Gateway-->>Merchant: {status: approved, auth_code}
  Merchant-->>Customer: Order confirmed

  Note over Gateway,Network: Settlement runs in batch T+1
  Gateway->>Network: Batch settlement file
  Network->>IssuerBank: Debit cardholder account
  IssuerBank->>Ledger: Post transaction
  Network-->>Gateway: Settlement confirmation
  Gateway-->>Merchant: Funds deposited

  Client->>GW: POST /process-request {payload}
  GW->>GW: Authenticate & rate-limit
  GW->>PP: Forward validated request

  activate PP
  PP->>ORA: SELECT product config, limits, rates
  ORA-->>PP: Product rules + rate tables

  PP->>RL: Evaluate eligibility rules(context)
  activate RL
  RL->>ORA: Fetch business rules v2.4
  ORA-->>RL: Rule set
  RL-->>PP: PASS / FAIL + reason codes
  deactivate RL

  PP->>PP: Calculate: fees, interest, tax, FX

  PP->>MQ: Publish calc-request to CALC.QUEUE
  activate EXT
  MQ->>EXT: Deliver computation message
  EXT->>EXT: Run actuarial / pricing model
  EXT->>MQ: Reply to CALC.REPLY.QUEUE
  MQ-->>PP: Computation result
  deactivate EXT

  PP->>PP: Merge external result + internal calc
  PP->>ORA: INSERT transaction_ledger (ACID)
  PP->>MDB: Append audit event (async)
  PP->>KFK: Publish domain event: ProcessingComplete

  PP-->>GW: 200 OK {result, txn_id, breakdown}
  deactivate PP
  GW-->>Client: Response with computed result

  Note over KFK: Downstream consumers
  Note over KFK: (Reporting, Notifications,
  Note over KFK: Reconciliation) pick up event

name: Periodic System Sync & Reconciliation
description: Scheduled background sync process that keeps multiple systems consistent — rsync-style data refresh, delta reconciliation, conflict detection, and rollback.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant SCH as Scheduler / Cron
  participant SYNC as Sync Orchestrator
  participant SRC as Source System (A)
  participant TGT as Target System (B)
  participant ORA as Oracle — Master
  participant MDB as MongoDB — Cache
  participant KFK as Kafka — Events
  participant MON as Monitoring

  SCH->>SYNC: TRIGGER sync job (04:00 UTC daily)
  activate SYNC

  SYNC->>MON: Log: sync_started {job_id, timestamp}
  SYNC->>ORA: SELECT MAX(last_sync_ts) FROM sync_registry
  ORA-->>SYNC: last_sync_ts = T-24h

  par Delta extraction
    SYNC->>SRC: GET /delta?since=T-24h&page=1
    SRC-->>SYNC: Records batch 1 (500 rows)
    SYNC->>SRC: GET /delta?since=T-24h&page=2
    SRC-->>SYNC: Records batch 2 (312 rows)
  and Cache refresh
    SYNC->>MDB: Drop stale entries (ttl < now)
    MDB-->>SYNC: 1,240 docs purged
  end

  SYNC->>SYNC: Compute checksums & diff
  SYNC->>TGT: POST /bulk-upsert {records, checksum}
  activate TGT
  TGT->>TGT: Validate + apply changes
  TGT->>ORA: MERGE INTO tgt_table (upsert)
  ORA-->>TGT: 812 rows affected
  TGT-->>SYNC: 200 OK {applied:812, skipped:0, errors:0}
  deactivate TGT

  SYNC->>SYNC: Reconcile: compare src vs tgt counts
  alt Counts match — clean sync
    SYNC->>ORA: UPDATE sync_registry SET last_sync_ts=NOW()
    SYNC->>KFK: Publish sync.completed {delta:812}
    SYNC->>MON: Log: sync_success
  else Mismatch detected
    SYNC->>MON: ALERT: reconciliation_mismatch
    SYNC->>KFK: Publish sync.failed {reason:'mismatch'}
    SYNC->>TGT: POST /rollback {job_id}
    TGT-->>SYNC: Rollback OK
    SYNC->>ORA: INSERT sync_errors log
  end

  SYNC->>MDB: Refresh cache with fresh TGT snapshot
  deactivate SYNC
  MON->>MON: Dashboard updated

name: Kafka Event-Driven Integration
description: Producer publishes a domain event to Kafka; multiple downstream consumers process independently — with dead-letter handling and offset management.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant SVC as Originating Service
  participant KFK as Kafka Broker
  participant C1 as Consumer: Ledger Svc
  participant C2 as Consumer: Fraud Engine
  participant C3 as Consumer: Notification Svc
  participant DLQ as Dead-Letter Topic
  participant MON as Schema Registry

  SVC->>MON: Validate event schema (Avro v3)
  MON-->>SVC: Schema OK — fingerprint: ab2f
  SVC->>KFK: Produce → topic: payment.processed
    Note right of KFK: partition by account_id
    Note right of KFK: retention: 7 days

  par Consumer Group: ledger-grp
    KFK-->>C1: Event (offset 14082)
    C1->>C1: Parse + validate payload
    C1->>C1: Post journal entries
    C1->>KFK: Commit offset 14082
  and Consumer Group: fraud-grp
    KFK-->>C2: Event (offset 14082)
    C2->>C2: Run ML fraud scoring
    alt Score > threshold
      C2->>KFK: Produce → topic: fraud.alerts
    else Score OK
      C2->>KFK: Commit offset 14082
    end
  and Consumer Group: notify-grp
    KFK-->>C3: Event (offset 14082)
    C3->>C3: Render template
    alt Delivery fails (3 retries)
      C3->>DLQ: Produce → topic: notify.dlq
      Note right of DLQ: Manual review
    else Delivery OK
      C3->>KFK: Commit offset 14082
    end
  end

  Note over KFK: Log compaction keeps
  Note over KFK: latest state per key

name: IBM MQ Request-Reply Pattern
description: Classic IBM MQ request-reply pattern between two enterprise systems — correlation IDs, reply-to queues, timeout and poison message handling.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant APP_A as Requester (System A)
  participant QM as IBM MQ Queue Manager
  participant REQ_Q as REQUEST.QUEUE
  participant REP_Q as REPLY.QUEUE
  participant APP_B as Responder (System B)
  participant DLQ as DEAD.LETTER.QUEUE

  APP_A->>APP_A: Build MQ message
    Note right of APP_A: MsgId: uuid-4a9f
    Note right of APP_A: CorrelId: corr-8b1e
    Note right of APP_A: ReplyToQ: REPLY.QUEUE
    Note right of APP_A: Expiry: 30000ms

  APP_A->>QM: MQPUT → REQUEST.QUEUE
  QM->>REQ_Q: Persist message (persistent delivery)
  REQ_Q-->>APP_A: MQPUT OK (msgId confirmed)

  QM-->>APP_B: MQGET (blocking / wait)
  activate APP_B
  APP_B->>APP_B: Parse request + validate schema
  APP_B->>APP_B: Execute business logic
  APP_B->>APP_B: Build reply message
    Note right of APP_B: CorrelId = original MsgId

  APP_B->>QM: MQPUT → REPLY.QUEUE (CorrelId set)
  deactivate APP_B
  QM->>REP_Q: Persist reply

  APP_A->>QM: MQGET(REPLY.QUEUE, CorrelId=uuid-4a9f)
  alt Reply received in time
    REP_Q-->>APP_A: Reply message
    APP_A->>APP_A: Process result
  else Expiry exceeded (30s)
    QM->>DLQ: Move expired message
    APP_A->>APP_A: Timeout → retry or escalate
    Note over DLQ: Ops team notified
  end

  Note over QM: MQ channel encryption: TLS 1.2+
  Note over QM: Channel auth: MQCSP / LDAP

name: SFTP File Exchange & Processing
description: End-to-end SFTP-based B2B file exchange — drop, detect, validate, process, archive, and acknowledgement flow including error quarantine.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant EXT as External Partner
  participant SFTP as SFTP Server
  participant WATCH as File Watcher / Poller
  participant VAL as Validator Service
  participant PROC as File Processor
  participant ORA as Oracle DB
  participant KFK as Kafka
  participant ACK as Ack / Notifier
  participant QUAR as Quarantine Store

  EXT->>SFTP: sftp PUT /inbound/payments_20240615.csv
  Note right of SFTP: File lands in /inbound/
  Note right of SFTP: Permissions: 640, owner: svc-sftp

  WATCH->>SFTP: Poll /inbound/ every 60s (or inotify)
  SFTP-->>WATCH: New file detected: payments_20240615.csv
  WATCH->>WATCH: Check file stable (size unchanged 5s)
  WATCH->>SFTP: Move file → /inbound/processing/

  WATCH->>VAL: Submit file path for validation
  activate VAL
  VAL->>VAL: Check: file size, encoding (UTF-8)
  VAL->>VAL: Validate header row + column count
  VAL->>VAL: Schema check: date, amount, IBAN formats
  alt Validation PASS
    VAL-->>PROC: File OK — begin processing
  else Validation FAIL
    VAL->>SFTP: Move → /inbound/quarantine/
    VAL->>QUAR: Log error report
    VAL->>ACK: Notify partner: REJECTED + reason
    ACK->>EXT: Email / API: validation_failed.json
  end
  deactivate VAL

  activate PROC
  PROC->>PROC: Parse CSV → record objects (chunked)
  loop Each batch of 1000 records
    PROC->>ORA: BULK INSERT INTO staging_payments
    PROC->>ORA: CALL sp_validate_and_post(batch_id)
    ORA-->>PROC: {posted:998, rejected:2, errors:[...]}
    PROC->>KFK: Publish payment.file.batch.processed
  end
  PROC->>SFTP: Move file → /inbound/archive/2024/06/
  PROC->>SFTP: Write ack: payments_20240615.ack
  deactivate PROC

  ACK->>EXT: Deliver acknowledgement file via SFTP
  Note over SFTP: Archive retained 90 days
  Note over SFTP: GPG encryption optional

name: Multi-System Transactional Computation
description: Complex multi-hop computation: System A → Product Processor → delegates to N specialist systems → aggregates results → responds. Covers Oracle, MongoDB, Kafka, REST, and async callbacks.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor CLIENT as Client / Consumer
  participant API as API Layer
  participant PP as Product Processor
  participant ORA as Oracle — Config & Ledger
  participant PRICE as Pricing Engine
  participant RISK as Risk Calculator
  participant TAX as Tax Engine
  participant FX as FX Rate Service
  participant KFK as Kafka
  participant MDB as MongoDB — Results Cache

  CLIENT->>API: POST /calculate {product, amount, currency, params}
  API->>PP: Validated request
  activate PP

  PP->>ORA: Fetch product config, fee schedule, limits
  ORA-->>PP: Config snapshot v12.3

  par Parallel specialist calculations
    PP->>PRICE: RPC calcPrice(product, volume, tier)
    activate PRICE
    PRICE->>ORA: SELECT price_matrix WHERE product=?
    ORA-->>PRICE: Price bands
    PRICE-->>PP: base_price=142.50, discount=12.00
    deactivate PRICE
  and
    PP->>RISK: RPC assessRisk(client_id, amount)
    activate RISK
    RISK->>MDB: Find client risk profile
    MDB-->>RISK: score=720, tier=B
    RISK-->>PP: risk_premium=4.25, approved=true
    deactivate RISK
  and
    PP->>FX: GET /rate?from=USD&to=GBP
    FX-->>PP: rate=0.7842, ts=now, source=ECB
  end

  PP->>TAX: calcTax(net=134.75, jurisdiction=GB)
  activate TAX
  TAX->>TAX: Apply VAT + withholding rules
  TAX-->>PP: vat=26.95, total_tax=26.95
  deactivate TAX

  PP->>PP: Aggregate:
    Note right of PP: gross     = 142.50
    Note right of PP: discount  = -12.00
    Note right of PP: risk_prem = +4.25
    Note right of PP: fx_adj    = *0.7842
    Note right of PP: tax       = +26.95
    Note right of PP: net_total = 134.71 GBP

  PP->>ORA: INSERT transaction (ACID, isolation=SERIALIZABLE)
  PP->>MDB: Cache result {txn_id, breakdown} TTL=300s
  PP->>KFK: Publish: calculation.completed {txn_id}

  PP-->>API: 200 {txn_id, net_total:134.71, breakdown}
  deactivate PP
  API-->>CLIENT: Final computed response

name: User Story: Payment Initiation
description: User story → system design: 'As a customer I want to initiate a payment so that my beneficiary receives funds.' Full backend flow from UI to settlement.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor U as 👤 Customer
  participant FE as Frontend App
  participant GW as API Gateway
  participant AUTH as Auth Service
  participant PAY as Payment Service
  participant PP as Product Processor
  participant FRAUD as Fraud Engine
  participant BANK as Core Banking

  Note over U,NOTIF: AC2 - Fraud checked before processing
  Note over U,NOTIF: AC3 - Customer notified on completion

  U->>FE: Enter payee, amount, reference
  FE->>GW: POST /payments {src_acct, dst_acct, amount, ccy}
  GW->>AUTH: Validate JWT + scopes

name: Microservice Saga Pattern
description: Distributed saga for a long-running transaction spanning multiple microservices — with compensating transactions on failure, Kafka choreography, and Oracle state tracking.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant CLI as Client
  participant ORCH as Saga Orchestrator
  participant KFK as Kafka
  participant INV as Inventory Svc
  participant PAY as Payment Svc
  participant SHIP as Shipping Svc
  participant ORA as Oracle — Saga State

  CLI->>ORCH: POST /orders {items, payment_method}
  ORCH->>ORA: INSERT saga_instance (status=STARTED)

  Note over ORCH,ORA: Step 1 — Reserve Inventory
  ORCH->>KFK: Publish → inventory.reserve.cmd
  KFK-->>INV: Consume reserve command
  INV->>INV: Check stock, place soft reserve
  alt Stock available
    INV->>KFK: Publish → inventory.reserved.evt
    KFK-->>ORCH: inventory.reserved.evt
    ORCH->>ORA: UPDATE saga: step1=DONE
  else Out of stock
    INV->>KFK: Publish → inventory.reserve.failed
    KFK-->>ORCH: inventory.reserve.failed
    ORCH->>ORA: UPDATE saga: FAILED at step1
    ORCH-->>CLI: 409 Out of stock
  end

  Note over ORCH,ORA: Step 2 — Charge Payment
  ORCH->>KFK: Publish → payment.charge.cmd
  KFK-->>PAY: Consume charge command
  PAY->>PAY: Authorise and capture payment
  alt Payment success
    PAY->>KFK: Publish → payment.charged.evt
    KFK-->>ORCH: payment.charged.evt
    ORCH->>ORA: UPDATE saga: step2=DONE
  else Payment declined
    PAY->>KFK: Publish → payment.failed
    KFK-->>ORCH: payment.failed
    ORCH->>KFK: Publish → inventory.release.cmd (COMPENSATE)
    KFK-->>INV: Release reservation
    ORCH->>ORA: UPDATE saga: COMPENSATED
    ORCH-->>CLI: 402 Payment declined
  end

  Note over ORCH,ORA: Step 3 — Create Shipment
  ORCH->>KFK: Publish → shipping.create.cmd
  KFK-->>SHIP: Consume shipment command
  SHIP->>SHIP: Allocate carrier, generate tracking
  SHIP->>KFK: Publish → shipping.created.evt
  KFK-->>ORCH: shipping.created.evt
  ORCH->>ORA: UPDATE saga: status=COMPLETED
  ORCH-->>CLI: 201 Order confirmed {order_id, tracking}

name: Data Sync State Machine
description: State machine for a data synchronisation process — idle, extracting, validating, loading, reconciling, and error/rollback states.
code:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Idle : System startup

  Idle --> Extracting : Schedule trigger / manual start
  note right of Idle
    Waiting for next
    sync window
    (cron: 0 4 * * *)
  end note

  Extracting --> Validating : Delta records fetched
  Extracting --> Error : Source unavailable / timeout

  Validating --> Transforming : Schema & business rules passed
  Validating --> Quarantine : Validation failures > threshold

  Transforming --> Loading : Records mapped to target schema
  Transforming --> Error : Transformation exception

  Loading --> Reconciling : Bulk upsert complete
  Loading --> RollingBack : DB constraint / deadlock

  Reconciling --> Idle : Counts match — sync OK
  Reconciling --> RollingBack : Count mismatch detected

  RollingBack --> Idle : Rollback committed
  RollingBack --> ManualIntervention : Rollback also failed

  Quarantine --> ManualIntervention : Notify ops team
  Error --> Retrying : Attempt ≤ 3
  Error --> ManualIntervention : Attempt > 3

  Retrying --> Extracting : Backoff delay elapsed
  ManualIntervention --> Idle : Ops team resolved
  ManualIntervention --> [*] : Abort sync job
